steps.title: Understand the exponential growth model
steps.content: In the function $S(n) = 38000 \cdot a^n$, the parameter $a$ is the annual growth factor (multiplier per year). We need to find the value of $a$ that represents a 4% annual salary increase.

steps.title: Test choice C (1.04)
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C (1.04): If $a = 1.04$, each year the salary is multiplied by 1.04.

This represents a 4% increase because $1.04 = 1 + 0.04$, where 0.04 is the decimal form of 4%.

So $S(1) = 38000 \cdot 1.04$ gives a 4% increase from the starting salary ✓.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
